transcript: Cool. Uh, thank you all for coming out. Um, last day of Breakpoint. It's been... it's been an honor, um, to have the chance to speak here today. Today I'll be talking about something that we spend a lot of time thinking about, which is: How do we get to the first trillion dollar dapp and billions of users onchain? One of the patterns that we see in technology is that when unit costs decrease and capabilities increase, entirely new applications become possible. This pattern is most apparent with the internet, with costs coming down 99.9% and usage increasing 7,000x since the 1990s. This phenomenon is commonly referred to as Jevons Paradox. Um, Jevons Paradox is... occurs when efficiency improvements lower the cost of a resource, product, or good, which can increase the overall demand of that resource or product such that the total consumption actually rises rather than falling. But a few conditions have to be true in order for this to occur. First, there's a technological change that increases the efficiency or productivity, which translates into lower prices for consumers and businesses. And that reduced price drastically increases the quantity demanded for that good or service. It normally increases drastically because these lower costs unlock new use cases that were literally impossible on the previous technology stack. This is also true for crypto. New onchain applications emerge when costs decrease and capabilities increase. And as we look at the application landscape today, it's remarkable because the highest revenue onchain apps today were made possible by L1s like Solana that have decreased costs and increased capabilities. Tether and Circle still lead the pack, but it's remarkable to see that so many of the top revenue-generating onchain apps today are uniquely enabled by L1s like Solana that allow developers to create application experiences that fulfill user needs that were previously too challenging or too costly to create. And to put it into context, Solana does more transactions at a lower cost than all... than all other major chains put together. But one of the things that's very dangerous with Jevons Paradox is we do have to answer the question of: Is this a race to the bottom? Is it actually value destruction? When we look at the long-term trend of network fees, we actually think that the pie is growing. Uh, networks like Solana provide users with applications that were previously not possible. And we're also seeing this on the onchain application revenue side. Um, uh, with onchain application revenue up 4x since 2021. So what does this mean going forward? One, we believe there is tremendous opportunity to onboard more users onchain. There are 300 million centralized exchange users who already hold crypto, doing at least one transaction a month, and we believe they're the easiest user base to convert. But one of the key questions is: Can onchain applications actually offer a competitive product? Most users today, when they onboard into crypto, they onboard onto a centralized exchange, but actually very few of them actually make it onchain. To try and answer this question, we can take a look at spot trading. Every single week, hundreds of thousands of new tokens are launched on Solana. Centralized exchanges historically have had a very lengthy listing process that makes it really hard for them to integrate these long-tail assets in a timely fashion. This is where the opportunity for the onchain apps are. Onchain DEXs have captured 33% of centralized exchange spot trading volume, which is a 5x growth in five years, driven by this long-tail market. And initially it might seem that hundreds of thousands of these long-tail tokens being launched each week wouldn't really add up to much, but if you think about it, um, there are billions of content... content posted on social networks every single day, and the monetizable value of each individual piece of content is quite low, but when you add it all together, you can get a tremendous aggregate, uh... uh, amount of fees and revenue. So what do we think about the future use cases? We actually think that the use cases that will emerge onchain will be doing what offchain rails cannot. And as we look forward, we... we see a lot of potential, uh... uh, tailwinds that will support this. Competition between stablecoin rails create alternative onboarding paths onchain that historically was gated by centralized exchanges. Second, OAuth and email embedded wallets make one-click onboarding possible. And third, the draft market structure legislation creates the necessary carve-outs and... and... and clarity for application developers to really commit to building in crypto. Thank you for... thank you for your time.

# Solana Breakpoint 2025: Key Announcements and Stories from Saturday
Day Three maintained the high expectations with announcements from Solflare, Solana Mobile, and Colosseum, among others.
[Pablo](https://solanafloor.com/author/pablo)
Copy link
* Published:
* Edited:
It was said that this year’s Breakpoint would be Solana’s biggest conference yet, and I’m sure none of the several thousand delegates that made their way to the Etihad Arena this week would disagree with that sentiment.
Saturday’s session featured a diverse set of updates highlighting Solana’s continued expansion across hardware, real-world assets, and institutional finance, as well as side events such as Predictify, hosted by[Kalshi](https://x.com/Kalshi?s=20).
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999766576091025438?s=20)> The last day of Breakpoint 2025 delivered a mix of hackathon results, product demonstrations, and strategic commitments that underscored the network’s growing role as a settlement layer for both digital and traditional assets.
Here’s what you missed if you’re not in Abu Dhabi:
## At Breakpoint
1. Colosseum has announced winners for its Cypherpunk hackathon
* *Unruggable, the Solana-native hardware wallet, is crowned grand champion.*
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999765402453467212?s=20)> 1. Uranium Digital demoed their product, which enables institutions to buy Uranium onchain for the first time.
* *Settlement is happening fully on Solana*
* *The entire crypto experience is abstracted*
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999744679441826044?s=20)> 1. RockawayX launches 3 vaults on Solana.
* *Risk-curated RWA vault on Kamino*
* *Additional vaults on Exponent Finance and MidasRWA*
* *Partners with Figure, Onre, Solstice, and Huma as professional curators for these vaults.*
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999753859456204968?s=20)> 1. Solana Mobile is expanding beyond its own devices to additional hardware.
* *Working with FXTech, MediaTek, and Trustonic to integrate the Solana Mobile stack at the chipset level for other Android devices.*
* *MediaTek ships roughly 50% of all Android devices globally.*
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999757725505642967?s=20)> 1. Splyce Finance introduces $SFULC on Solana.
* *$SFULC is a yield-bearing token offering 10% APY powered by Fulcrum Lending*
* *Powered by real cash flows from senior, secured multifamily credit.*
* *$SFULC can be minted with stablecoins or bought directly on Raydium.*
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999761140931526825?s=20)> 1. VC Firm 6th Man Ventures announces that it is focused on Solan

tags.impact_reason: Keynote discusses scaling solutions and cost reduction on Solana, highlighting its potential for attracting more users and dApps, but lacks concrete announcements.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a crystalline core under immense pressure as the Semantic Anchor to visualize 'Fee Compression'. The paradox of a shrinking cost leading to explosive growth (Jevons Paradox) is shown by the crystal not shattering, but instead unleashing a massive torrent of light and data. I integrated the text '7000X' as a holographic artifact within this energy explosion to diegetically represent the massive usage increase cited in the article.
visual_idea.description: A central, glowing crystalline cube, representing a single transaction fee, is caught between two massive, dark, polished obsidian pistons. The pistons are visibly compressing the crystal, causing micro-fractures of light to appear on its surface. Instead of shattering, the crystal unleashes an overwhelming explosion of holographic, fiber-optic light trails that surge upwards and outwards. Within this chaotic explosion of light, the number "7000X" is formed by the coalescing data streams, appearing as a glowing, holographic metric of the output. The background is a cavernous, dark industrial space, filled with machinery thrown into sharp relief by the central event.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A monumental, central, intensely glowing crystalline cube, visually representing a single transaction fee, is powerfully gripped between two colossal, dark, highly polished obsidian pistons within a vast, vertical composition. The pistons exert immense pressure, visibly compressing the crystal, which responds by developing intricate micro-fractures of brilliant light across its surface. Instead of shattering, the crystal erupts, unleashing an overwhelming, chaotic explosion of vibrant holographic, fiber-optic light trails that surge dramatically upwards and outwards, filling the cavernous space with raw energy. Amidst this incandescent chaos, the number "7000X" organically forms, coalescing from the surging data streams, manifesting as a brilliantly glowing, holographic metric of the immense output. The backdrop is a cavernous, sprawling dark industrial space, with colossal, intricate machinery looming in the deep shadows, dramatically thrown into sharp relief by the intense, high-contrast volumetric lighting emanating from the central event, casting sharp, well-defined shadows. The composition emphasizes immense height and epic scale, with significant negative space at the top, making the central event feel monumental. The scene is illuminated with high-contrast volumetric lighting, creating dramatic light rays, deep, well-defined shadows, and vibrant energy akin to a fusion reactor, showcasing the polished obsidian textures and the intricate crystalline structure with hyperrealistic detail. High dynamic range accentuates the intense glow and the subtle reflections. 3:4 aspect ratio, 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photorealistic, cinematic contrast, volumetric lighting, high dynamic range.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a crystalline core under immense pressure as the Semantic Anchor to visualize 'Fee Compression'. The paradox of a shrinking cost leading to explosive growth (Jevons Paradox) is shown by the crystal not shattering, but instead unleashing a massive torrent of light and data. I integrated the text '7000X' as a holographic artifact within this energy explosion to diegetically represent the massive usage increase cited in the article.
visual_idea.description: A macro, close-up shot focusing on the point of compression. We see the surface of the glowing crystalline core, its internal lattice structure glowing intensely under the immense pressure from the edge of a dark, metallic piston just entering the top of the frame. Wisps and tendrils of pure light and data begin to erupt from glowing micro-fractures, hinting at the massive energy release without showing the full explosion.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A perfectly symmetrical, centrally focused, tight macro close-up shot, filling the 1:1 square frame with texture. The scene focuses intensely on the point of immense compression. We observe the multifaceted surface of a glowing crystalline core, its intricate internal lattice structure emitting an intense, ethereal glow under the immense pressure. The edge of a dark, polished obsidian-like metallic piston, highly reflective and sleek, just enters the top of the frame, exerting crushing force. From glowing micro-fractures on the crystalline surface, wisps and tendrils of pure light and data begin to erupt, like contained fusion energy, hinting at a massive energy release without showing the full explosion. High-contrast volumetric lighting emanates from the core, creating dramatic light rays and deep, dark shadows, highlighting the sharp facets and reflective surfaces. The overall aesthetic is hyperrealistic with cinematic contrast. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, high dynamic range.

editorial.headline: Electric Capital: Solana Fee Compression Sparks "Jevons Paradox" Event
editorial.standfirst: General Partner Ren CF argues falling unit costs are not a race to the bottom but a precursor to a 7,000x usage explosion, citing a massive shift in DEX market share. The firm outlines the structural roadmap to crypto's first trillion-dollar application.
key_takeaways: Onchain DEXs have captured 33% of centralized exchange spot volume, a 5x increase over the last five years.
Onchain application revenue has quadrupled since 2021, defying concerns that low fees lead to value destruction.
The "Long Tail" of asset issuance—hundreds of thousands of new tokens weekly—is identified as the primary revenue driver for future dapps.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
In a data-heavy keynote at Breakpoint 2025, **Electric Capital** General Partner Ren CF presented a contrarian thesis on blockchain economics: the collapse of transaction costs on **Solana** is not a race to the bottom, but the catalyst for a "Jevons Paradox" event. Drawing a direct parallel to the internet infrastructure of the 1990s—where a 99.9% drop in costs spurred a 7,000x increase in usage—Ren argued that Solana’s efficiency is unlocking entirely new categories of demand. The firm contends that we are currently witnessing the early stages of this phenomenon, where the total consumption of blockspace rises faster than the price per transaction falls.

The smoking gun for this shift is found in liquidity formation. **Electric Capital** revealed that onchain Decentralized Exchanges (DEXs) have now captured **33% of centralized exchange spot trading volume**, marking a 5x growth trajectory over five years. This surge is driven by the "long tail" of assets—hundreds of thousands of new tokens launched weekly that centralized intermediaries cannot list fast enough. Ren compared this explosion of assets to user-generated content on social media: while the individual value of a single piece of content (or token) is low, the aggregate value of the network creates a massive revenue engine.

Looking ahead, the firm identified the specific infrastructure rails that will bridge the gap to the first "trillion-dollar dapp." With **300 million users** currently sitting on centralized exchanges, the transition to onchain activity will be facilitated by the convergence of stablecoin competition, OAuth-enabled embedded wallets, and clarifying market structure legislation. For institutional investors, the signal is clear: value accrual is moving away from the fat-protocol thesis and toward high-velocity applications that leverage Solana’s low-cost environment to aggregate millions of micro-transactions.
